items.title: Iraq, Qatar Stress Diplomacy as Key to Resolving Regional Crises
items.desc: Iraqi Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi and Qatar's Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani called for implementing the US-Iran memorandum of understanding as Baghdad also prepares for al-zaidi’s visit to Iran this week

items.title: Kurdish Politician Zebari: Iraqi PM's US Trip Marks New Chapter in Washington-Baghdad Ties
items.desc: Hoshyar Zebari says the trip's outcomes, including new memoranda on energy and finance, mark a defining moment for Iraq's relationship with the United States.

items.title: Rubio Praises Lebanon's Efforts to Disarm Hezbollah in Washington Talks
items.desc: Lebanon President's visit marks the first official trip by a Lebanese head of state to Washington since 2009, as the two countries discuss implementation of a framework aimed at ending the conflict between Israel and Hezbollah.

items.title: Iran's FM Tells his Iraqi Counterpart, Ties Between Tehran and Baghdad Are 'Strategic and Deep'
items.desc: Iranian foreign minister says bilateral relations should remain unaffected by personal remarks as Tehran stresses continued coordination with Baghdad amid regional tensions

items.title: Iranian FM: Strait of Hormuz is Tehran's 'Real Atomic Bomb'
items.desc: Abbas Araghchi says the recent 12-day conflict with Israel and the United States was merely the opening chapter of a longer confrontation, as he defends Tehran's refusal to formally suspend uranium enrichment

items.title: 5+ Iraqi Airways executives suspended over missing capital
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Ministry of Transport suspended eight senior Iraqi Airways officials on Sunday as authorities investigate the disappearance of 16 billion Iraqi dinars ($12.2M) from the state-owned carrier's accounts. An official document showed that Transport Minister Wahab Salman al-Hassani ordered the suspensions under Iraq's State Employees Discipline Law, pending the outcome of the

items.title: Kuwait condemns Iranian strike on power, water facility
items.desc: Shafaq News- Kuwait Kuwait held Iran fully responsible on Sunday for an attack on a power generation and water desalination plant, warning of the legal, moral, and security consequences of the strike. In a statement, the Foreign Ministry noted that the repeated deliberate targeting of critical civilian infrastructure marks a dangerous escalation and poses a serious threat to civilian safety and se

items.title: Integrity Commission searches for buried cash in Baghdad farms
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq's Integrity Commission raided farms owned by Services Alliance (Khadamat) leader Shibl Al-Zaidi and former Labor Minister and Coordination Framework leader Ahmed Al-Asadi in Baghdad on Sunday, a security source told Shafaq News. The raid on Al-Zaidi's farm was part of a search for money suspected to be buried on the property, the source said. A second team simultaneously

items.title: Official preparations begin for PM Al-Zaidi Tehran visit
items.desc: Shafaq News- Baghdad Iraq’s Prime Minister Ali al-Zaidi will visit Tehran next week for talks on bilateral relations and the signing of several memorandums of understanding covering areas of cooperation, the Foreign Ministry confirmed on Sunday. Deputy Foreign Minister for Bilateral Relations Mohammed Hussein Bahr Al-Uloum has already discussed preparations for the visit with Iran’s am

items.title: Iran claims second US drone downed in one day
items.desc: Shafaq News- Tehran Iran's army said on Sunday it had shot down a "hostile" MQ-9 Reaper drone over western Iran. Earlier today, the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) reported intercepting and destroying another MQ-9 drone over the southern province of Bushehr. The United States has not commented on either claim. The US Central Command (CENTCOM) continued its military campaign against Iran,

items.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
items.desc: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

items.title: Tom Cruise, Jennifer Hudson among star appearances at World Cup closing ceremony
items.desc: The celebrity line-up will continue at half-time with performances by Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and BTS.

items.title: Health officials identify source of US explosive diarrhoea outbreak
items.desc: The US Food and Drug Administration says iceberg lettuce supplied by Taylor Farms and sold at Taco Bell and Walmart locations was the source of cyclosporiasis infections.

items.title: Grandmother, 82, wins law change so she can enjoy Happy Hour at care home
items.desc: Anita Le Brun, the face of a campaign to allow for alcohol to be served at care homes without a licence, is celebrating its success.

items.title: Two US troops killed and one missing after Iranian attack in Jordan
items.desc: Iran and the US have traded strikes as hostilities re-escalated this week, with Tehran targeting US military facilities across the Gulf region.

items.title: Who is Andy Burnham, the U.K.'s new prime minister?
items.desc: On Monday, former Manchester Mayor Andy Burnham succeeds Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister. Burnham's politics have been shaped by England's north-south class divide.

items.title: An Israeli paraglider tries to save migrating swifts that nest in the Western Wall
items.desc: Swifts have nested in Jerusalem's Western Wall since biblical times. Now one Israeli paraglider is trying to save them.

items.title: Deadly overnight Russian attack on Ukraine targets the capital
items.desc: Russia launched 41 missiles and 125 attack drones across Ukraine overnight, according to the Ukrainian air force.

items.title: Cuban artist and dissident exiled from country after 5 years in prison arrives in US
items.desc: A famous Cuban dissident artist and musician, Luis Manuel Otero Alcántara, arrived in Miami on Saturday after being released from a five-year prison sentence on the condition that he leave his country.

items.title: Andrew and Tristan Tate arrested in Florida on charges of rape and sex trafficking
items.desc: Influencer Andrew Tate and his brother Tristan have been arrested in Miami after British authorities issued 38 new charges against them, and said they are "seeking extradition" for the two men.

items.title: US and Iran trade fire after two US soldiers killed in Jordan
items.desc: Kuwait says an electricity and water plant is on fire after the second such attack in two days. Iran says US strikes hit an under-construction nuclear power plant.

items.title: Second tanker in three months hijacked off Yemen by suspected Somali pirates
items.desc: There have also been several unsuccessful attacks in a region where piracy appears to be making a comeback.

items.title: US strikes oil tanker with missiles as it enforces new Iran blockade
items.desc: The US military targeted an unladen oil tanker which it said was heading for the key Iranian oil terminal on Kharg island.

items.title: US launches fresh strikes on Iran as Trump warns Tehran it 'better behave'
items.desc: US President Donald Trump says he is yet to decide whether or not he will "finish off" Iran.

items.title: Trump threatens to bomb bridges and power plants unless Iran resumes talks
items.desc: Trump's comments aired as the two countries exchanged fire for the fourth day in a row and the US resumed blockading Iranian ports.
